questions.questionText: Lines AB and CD are parallel. Line EF is a transversal intersecting AB at E and CD at F. If $\angle AEF$ = 65°, what is the measure of $\angle EFD$?
questions.explanation: $\angle AEF$ and $\angle EFD$ are consecutive interior angles. For parallel lines, consecutive interior angles are supplementary, so their sum is 180°. Therefore, $\angle EFD$ = 180° - 65° = 115°.

questions.questionText: In triangle PQR, PQ = PR. $\angle QPR$ = 40°. What is the measure of $\angle PQR$?
questions.explanation: Since PQ = PR, triangle PQR is an isosceles triangle. The base angles are equal, so $\angle PQR$ = $\angle PRQ$. The sum of angles in a triangle is 180°. So, 2 * $\angle PQR$ + 40° = 180°. 2 * $\angle PQR$ = 140°, which means $\angle PQR$ = 70°.

questions.questionText: A regular hexagon has an interior angle sum of 720°. What is the measure of each interior angle of the hexagon?
questions.explanation: A regular hexagon has 6 equal interior angles. To find the measure of each interior angle, divide the sum of interior angles by the number of sides: 720° / 6 = 120°.

questions.questionText: In a quadrilateral ABCD, angle A = 90°, angle B = 80°, and angle C = 110°. What is the measure of angle D?
questions.explanation: The sum of interior angles in a quadrilateral is 360°. So, 90° + 80° + 110° + angle D = 360°. 280° + angle D = 360°. Angle D = 360° - 280° = 70°.

questions.questionText: A circle has a tangent AB at point P. O is the centre of the circle. If $\angle AOP$ = 50°, what is the measure of $\angle OPB$?
questions.explanation: The radius drawn to the point of tangency is perpendicular to the tangent. Therefore, $\angle OPB$ (the angle between the radius OP and the tangent AB) is 90°.

questions.questionText: In a cyclic quadrilateral ABCD, angle A = 70°. What is the measure of angle C?
questions.explanation: In a cyclic quadrilateral, opposite angles are supplementary. Therefore, angle A + angle C = 180°. So, 70° + angle C = 180°. Angle C = 180° - 70° = 110°.

questions.questionText: Two parallel lines are intersected by a transversal. If one of the interior angles on the same side of the transversal is 130°, what is the measure of the other interior angle on the same side?
questions.explanation: Consecutive interior angles (or co-interior angles) are supplementary, meaning they add up to 180°. If one angle is 130°, the other is 180° - 130° = 50°.

questions.questionText: The exterior angle of a regular polygon is 40°. How many sides does the polygon have?
questions.explanation: For any regular polygon, the sum of its exterior angles is 360°. If each exterior angle is 40°, the number of sides (n) is 360° / 40° = 9 sides.

questions.questionText: In triangle XYZ, angle X = 60° and angle Y = 70°. What type of triangle is XYZ?
questions.explanation: First, find angle Z: 180° - (60° + 70°) = 180° - 130° = 50°. Since all three angles (60°, 70°, 50°) are different, all three sides must also be different lengths, making it a scalene triangle.

questions.questionText: An angle at the centre of a circle is 110°. What is the angle at the circumference subtended by the same arc?
questions.explanation: The angle at the centre is twice the angle at the circumference when both are subtended by the same arc. So, the angle at the circumference is 110° / 2 = 55°.

questions.questionText: Lines GH and IJ are parallel. $\angle GHK$ = 125°. Line HK is a transversal. What is the measure of $\angle HKI$?
questions.explanation: $\angle GHK$ and $\angle HKI$ are consecutive interior angles between parallel lines. They are supplementary, so $\angle HKI$ = 180° - 125° = 55°.

questions.questionText: In a parallelogram ABCD, $\angle DAB$ = 100°. What is the measure of $\angle ABC$?
questions.explanation: In a parallelogram, consecutive angles are supplementary. Therefore, $\angle DAB$ + $\angle ABC$ = 180°. So, 100° + $\angle ABC$ = 180°. $\angle ABC$ = 180° - 100° = 80°.
